document.source_title: Western Withdrawal, Jihadist Expansion: How the Sahel Became Ground Zero for Global Terrorism
document.summary: The Sahel has become the epicenter of global terrorism, with a significant increase in attacks and fatalities attributed to jihadist groups like Jama’at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin. Recent coordinated assaults across Mali highlight the deteriorating security situation exacerbated by the withdrawal of Western forces and the rise of Russian influence. The region now accounts for a substantial portion of global terrorism-related deaths, reflecting a shift in the dynamics of insurgency and governance.
key_takeaways: Jama’at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin has become the dominant jihadist group in the Sahel.
The Sahel accounts for roughly half of all terrorism-related deaths worldwide.
Western military presence in the Sahel has collapsed, leading to increased insurgent activity.
Russian forces have not stabilized the region and have faced significant setbacks.
The April 2026 attacks demonstrate a coordinated effort by jihadist and separatist forces.
The situation in Mali reflects a broader trend of governance failure and insurgent expansion.
document.why_it_matters: The research highlights the consequences of Western withdrawal from the Sahel, illustrating how it has created a power vacuum exploited by jihadist groups. Understanding these dynamics is crucial for developing effective counter-terrorism strategies and addressing the underlying governance issues that fuel insurgency in the region.

claims.claim_text: The Sahel now accounts for roughly half of all terrorism-related deaths worldwide.
claims.evidence_text: The Sahel now accounts for roughly half of all terrorism-related deaths worldwide, a dramatic shift from just over a decade ago.

claims.claim_text: Jama’at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin has emerged as the dominant jihadist actor in the Sahel.
claims.evidence_text: Jamaat Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin has emerged as the dominant jihadist actor in the Sahel, outpacing Islamic State in the Greater Sahara.

claims.claim_text: Western military presence and influence across the central Sahel have collapsed.
claims.evidence_text: Over the past three years, Western military presence and influence across the central Sahel have collapsed.

entities.name: Jama’at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin
entities.description: A dominant jihadist group in the Sahel, formed in 2017 as an al-Qaeda-affiliated coalition.
entities.evidence_text: Jamaat Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin has emerged as the dominant jihadist actor in the Sahel.

entities.name: Mali
entities.description: A country in the Sahel that has experienced significant jihadist violence and military instability.
entities.evidence_text: The attacks targeted military installations and strategic sites across Mali.

entities.name: Burkina Faso
entities.description: A country in the Sahel that expelled French forces and has seen increased jihadist activity.
entities.evidence_text: Burkina Faso followed in 2023, expelling French forces.

entities.name: Niger
entities.description: A country in the Sahel where the U.S. completed its withdrawal in September 2024.
entities.evidence_text: The United States completed its withdrawal from Niger in September 2024.

events.event: Coordinated attacks against military installations in Mali
events.evidence_text: On April 25, armed groups launched near-simultaneous attacks against military installations and key strategic sites across Mali.
French forces withdrew from Mali in 2022 after the military junta demanded its exit.
Burkina Faso followed in 2023, expelling French forces.
The United States completed its withdrawal from Niger in September 2024.

metrics.name: terrorism-related deaths
metrics.evidence_text: Terrorism-related deaths have increased nearly tenfold since 2019, with almost 20,000 fatalities recorded over that period.

metrics.name: increase in violence involving Russian mercenaries
metrics.evidence_text: Violence involving Russian mercenaries in Mali has increased 81 percent, with a 65 percent increase in reported fatalities in recent years.

relationships.subject: Jama’at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin
relationships.evidence_text: Claimed by Jama’at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin, a jihadist group, and conducted in coordination with Tuareg separatist forces.

relationships.subject: Western governments
relationships.relationship: have diminished capacity to respond to
relationships.evidence_text: Western governments now face a predicament of their own making.

citations.title: Western Withdrawal, Jihadist Expansion: How the Sahel Became Ground Zero for Global Terrorism

source_credibility.reason: The source provides detailed analysis and data on the evolving security situation in the Sahel, supported by credible evidence.
